---
title: "Research"
---

::: {.page-header}
<div class="kicker">Research</div>
# Health economics, preference evidence and behaviour
My research develops and applies methods for understanding how people value health, process information, and make trade-offs under uncertainty.
:::

::: {.content}
::: {.grid-2}
::: {.card}
### Health economics and policy
Economic evaluation, health technology assessment, public health policy, health service design, vaccine policy, and policy acceptability.
:::
::: {.card}
### Choice modelling
Discrete choice experiments, mixed logit, latent class models, preference heterogeneity, attribute non-attendance, equity-efficiency trade-offs, and willingness-to-pay or willingness-to-sacrifice measures.
:::
::: {.card}
### Behavioural health economics
Attention, numeracy, feedback, presentation effects, eye tracking, task engagement, information processing, and decision quality in health preference tasks.
:::
::: {.card}
### Decision support
Translation of statistical and econometric evidence into transparent online tools for policy evaluation, clinical decision support, and applied economic evaluation.
:::
:::

## Current research streams

::: {.grid-3}
::: {.card}
### Vaccine mandate evaluation
Mandate design, exemptions, scope, thresholds, policy legitimacy, predicted support, and decision support tools for future public health emergencies.
:::
::: {.card}
### Eye tracking and DCEs
How visual attention shapes stated preferences, cost sensitivity, response quality and the interpretation of attribute importance.
:::
::: {.card}
### Equity and value assessment
How people trade off total health gain against priority to worse-off groups, fairness and distributional concerns.
:::
::: {.card}
### Telehealth and access
Preferences for telehealth models, continuity, distance, waiting time, data security and out-of-pocket costs.
:::
::: {.card}
### Rare disease care
Organisational models of care, coordination, access to innovation, continuity and patient-centred service design.
:::
::: {.card}
### Applied benefit-cost analysis
Economic tools for applied decision-making in agriculture, public policy and programme evaluation.
:::
:::
:::
